original_abstract:
This research explores the philosophical foundations and ontological dimensions of the educational thought of TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid, specifically focusing on the development of Islamic boarding schools (pesantren). Utilizing a qualitative descriptive methodology and a descriptive analysis approach, the study evaluates the origin and essence of his ideas and their continued relevance within the modern Indonesian educational landscape. The findings reveal that Abdul Madjid’s educational framework was significantly shaped by his lineage and his mentorship under prominent scholars at the Madrasah Shaulatiyah in Mecca, including Syaikh Hasan Muhammad al-Masysyath and Syaikh Salim Rahmatullah. His contributions include pioneering classical Islamic education systems, establishing specialized institutions for women, and integrating the Ahl al-Sunnah wa al-Jama’ah doctrine into formal curricula to counter colonial and extremist influences. The study highlights his success in transforming the social and spiritual fabric of the Lombok community through innovative concepts and practices. Furthermore, the paper discusses the contemporary challenges facing pesantren—such as globalization and the need for professional teacher welfare—arguing that Abdul Madjid’s emphasis on sincerity, teacher integrity, and ideological clarity remains a vital reference point for navigating current pedagogical shifts in Indonesia.

**Keywords:** Ontology, TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid, Islamic education, Pesantren.
humanized_abstract:
PHILOSOPHY, EXISTENCE, AND MATRIX OF EDUCATIONAL THOUGHT OF TGKH. M. ZAINUDDIN ABDUL MADJID THROUGH THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE INDONESIAN PESANTREN The research tries to probe the philosophy and existence of educational thought of TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid through the development of the Indonesian Pusantren (Islamic boarding school). This study employs qualitative descriptive method with descriptive analysis approach. In the hope to explain deeply about the origin and soul of the Abdul Madjid’s educational thought and the correspondence and its relevance on the national education system in contemporary Indonesia. The result of the study revealed that a host of Abdul Madjid’s educational thoughts in terms of having an educational institution in the classical way from his ancestral line while in his time to study Islam in Mecca through Madrasah Shaulatiyah where he had mentors from the Islamic scholars in that time such as Syaikh Hasan Muhammad al-Masysyath and Syaikh Salim Rahmatullah. They are the Islamic scholar which is able to teach at that time, which is tasked with developing the educational system of IslamicPesantren, having developed several educational institutions especially for women such as Madrasah Ibtidaiyah for girls at 1896 and an education institution namely Al-Azhar for women students located at Pontren Big in Blanga. Then all the school and educational institutions are taught with concept and framework of Ahl al-Sunnah wa al-Jama’ah in order to ward-off impacts of colonialism in that time and as well to ward off extremist thought in contemporary Indonesia. Therefore TGKH. M Zainuddin Abdul Madjid succeeded to establish a change to the Lombok people towards the social as well as spiritual progress by proposing new theories and educational institutions. In contemporary era Pusantren and Indonesian teacher are having to fight against several challenges such as globalization and the problem of teachers’ welfare that Abdul Madjid had instilled his concepts such as honesty, teacher’s integrity as well as ideological integration as referential solution for handling the present-day educational change in Indonesia.

**Keywords:** Ontology, TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid, Islamic education, Pesantren.
